name: Via Transportation, Inc.
business: Via transforms antiquated and siloed public transportation systems into smart, data-driven, and AI-powered efficient digital networks. We offer a curated suite of technology-enabled services that enable customers to more easily adopt our software and benefit from lower-cost operations. Our customers are government agencies and private organizations responsible for providing public transportation.

exposures.sentence: The use of machine learning and artificial intelligence ( AI ) is intrinsic to our platform and underlies continuous improvement in the performance of our software.
Our software seamlessly integrates autonomous shuttles into the broader transportation landscape.

The use of technologies such as digital twins and generative-AI allows the product to offer proactive suggestions and cost-estimates to planners on more efficient adjustments to their networks based on the data from other alike cities globally.
we may expand our platform and pursue initiatives, including electric vehicle partnerships, autonomous vehicle partnerships, logistics, and advertising, which may not be successful.
Eliminates the need for costly on-premise solutions by offering a cloud-based platform.
Deep data insights communicated in a clear and digestible format that enables our customers to continuously optimize and report their performance.

catalysts.sentence: On September 15, 2025, the Company completed its IPO in which we issued and sold 7,142,857 shares of Class A common stock at $46.00 per share.
subsequently, on October 14, 2025, the underwriters of the IPO elected to exercise their over-allotment option to purchase an additional 1,358,236 shares of Class A common stock at the IPO Price.
On December 12, 2025, the Company acquired Downtowner Transportation LLC and all of its affiliated subsidiaries ("Downtowner") for cash consideration of $40.7 million.

Concurrently with the closing of our IPO, $53.3 million in principal and accrued contractual interest on the Convertible Notes automatically converted into 1,655,908 shares of the Company's Class A common stock based on a 30% discount to the IPO price.
In November 2025, we repaid in full the outstanding balance on our line of credit.
Patent Litigation costs relate to the RideCo litigation in which Via won a trial in January 2025.
the recording of non-cash revaluation adjustments of $2.3 million relating to an outstanding warrant to purchase shares of Series E preferred stock, which was exercised in February 2025.

tamClaims.claim: We are addressing a striking gap in the $545 billion global public transportation market.
tamClaims.sentence: We are addressing a striking gap in the $545 billion global public transportation market.

tamClaims.claim: We estimate the total addressable market in North America and Western Europe to be approximately 63,000 customers, based on a report commissioned by us from a major consulting firm.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate the total addressable market in North America and Western Europe to be approximately 63,000 customers, based on a report commissioned by us from a major consulting firm.
